title: PM Approval Detail Decision History Context
objective: Render compact read-only decision-history context inside PM approval detail workflows using the scoped reads introduced by PM Lane 014, without adding fetches, backend endpoints, schema changes, auth changes, ingress changes, or mutation authority.
orchestration_inputs: PM Lane 012 remains the deployment gate for hosted mutation-seam parity and live PM proof.
Read-only scout 019e2c92-20ed-7260-b16d-809f2d39ffde confirmed the smallest next slice is a display-only compact history renderer fed by data.history, placed before mutable decision controls.
Coordinator execution kept write ownership local to the PM approval frontend and focused approval-context smoke while using the scout as the parallel orchestration lane.
constrained_outputs: A reusable compact decision-history context renderer displays up to three scoped history rows with action, timestamp, state transition, actor, and reason.
Workpackage, task, snapshot, and active escalation approval detail panels render the compact history context before mutable decision/disposition controls.
No new decision-history fetch is added; the renderer consumes the data.history result produced by the PM Lane 014 route-derived endpoint.
Focused Playwright proof injects scoped decision-history fixture rows and asserts the compact context renders for escalation and snapshot detail screens.
Existing scoped-read assertions, tracer handoff, and schedule handoff remain covered by the same focused smoke.
validation_steps: CI=1 corepack pnpm --dir C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform --filter @apex/operations-web exec playwright test tests/browser-shell.approval-context.smoke.spec.ts
validation_results: focused approval-context Playwright proof passed 2/2 after rebuilding operations-web
initial focused Playwright attempt before the rebuild exercised stale built output and failed the new compact-history assertions; the rebuilt bundle passed
sql_permission_note: Packet 2026-05-15-pm-lane-015 is frontend-only and does not authorize SQL writes, schema migration, live data mutation, auth widening, ingress widening, schedule mutation, assignment mutation, Vercel promotion, or Render deployment action.
